message:
feat(wal,pager): add affine checksum transforms and frame scratch reuse

Introduce WalChecksumTransform, an affine representation of SQLite's
rolling WAL checksum that allows frame-local checksum work to be
precomputed before the authoritative publish-time seed is known. The
transform decomposes each frame's checksum contribution into linear
coefficients (a11, a12, a21, a22) and constant biases (c1, c2), so
append_prepared_frame_bytes can rebind to the live seed with a simple
matrix-vector multiply instead of re-scanning all frame bytes.

Also refactor single-frame and batch append paths to reuse a per-handle
frame_scratch buffer via std::mem::take, eliminating per-append heap
allocations for the common case. The scratch buffer is cleared on WAL
reset but retains its capacity for the next append cycle.

Key changes:
- checksum.rs: WalChecksumTransform with identity, from_aligned_bytes,
  for_wal_frame, apply, and then (composition); write_wal_frame_checksum_fields
  for writing pre-computed checksums
- wal.rs: append_frame and append_frames reuse frame_scratch; append_prepared_frame_bytes
  accepts &[WalChecksumTransform] and applies transforms instead of recomputing;
  test helpers for scratch introspection; Track C scratch benchmark harness
- traits.rs: PreparedWalChecksumTransform struct; checksum_transforms field on
  PreparedWalFrameBatch; append_prepared_frames takes &mut
- wal_adapter.rs: bridge PreparedWalChecksumTransform <-> WalChecksumTransform
- pager.rs: mutability adjustments for prepared batch; BlockingObservedLockVfs
  test infrastructure for publish-window measurement

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
